hypothesis: If LLM agents are given tools to design and run experiments and to retrieve observational data in both a rule-discovery parlor game (Alien Market) and a simulated ALP reactor, then they will uncover the true governing rules of each system—specifically that items containing the letters 'p' or 'm' are rejected in Alien Market and the underlying chemical reaction rules and favorable operating conditions in ALP—because their success depends on discovering causal rules through iterative experimentation, and this success will be greater
